package validator

import (
	"fmt"

	"example.com/app/repository"
)

// Positive scenario for validator_depends_on_repository_directly.
// Rule intent: Reusable validator code that reaches into repositories instead of
// receiving precomputed facts or focused services.

type UserValidator struct {
	userRepo *repository.UserRepository
}

func NewUserValidator(repo *repository.UserRepository) *UserValidator {
	return &UserValidator{userRepo: repo}
}

// ValidateEmailUniqueness directly queries the repository to check uniqueness.
// This couples the validator to the data layer.
func (v *UserValidator) ValidateEmailUniqueness(email string) error {
	exists, err := v.userRepo.ExistsByEmail(email)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to check email uniqueness: %w", err)
	}
	if exists {
		return fmt.Errorf("email %s is already taken", email)
	}
	return nil
}

// ValidateUsernameUniqueness also directly queries the repository.
func (v *UserValidator) ValidateUsernameUniqueness(username string) error {
	exists, err := v.userRepo.ExistsByUsername(username)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to check username uniqueness: %w", err)
	}
	if exists {
		return fmt.Errorf("username %s is already taken", username)
	}
	return nil
}

// ValidateUser performs full validation by calling repository methods directly.
func (v *UserValidator) ValidateUser(email, username string) error {
	if err := v.ValidateEmailUniqueness(email); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if err := v.ValidateUsernameUniqueness(username); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	return nil
}
